# terrible_wti_remote_cpm_serial_port_config (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cpm_password` (String) This is the Password of the WTI device to send the module.
- `cpm_url` (String) This is the URL of the WTI device to send the module.
- `cpm_username` (String) This is the Username of the WTI device to send the module.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `port` (Number) This is the port number that is getting the action performed on.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `baud` (Number) This is the baud rate to assign to the port. 0=300, 1=1200, 2=2400, 3=4800, 4=9600, 5=19200, 6=38400, 7=57600, 8=115200, 9=230400, 10=460800
- `break_allow` (Boolean) This is if the break character is allowed to be passed through the port, 0=Off, 1=On
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `cmd` (Number) This is the Admin Mode to assign to the port, 0=Deny, 1=Permit.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `echo` (Boolean) -This is the command echo parameter to assign to the port, 0=Off, 1=On
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `handshake` (Number) This is the handshake to assign to the port, 0=None, 1=XON/XOFF, 2=RTS/CTS, 3=Both.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `logoff` (String) This is the logout character to assign to the port If preceded by a ^ character, the sequence will be a control character. Used if seq is set to 0 or 1
- `mode` (Number) This is the port mode to assign to the port, 0=Any-to-Any. 1=Passive, 2=Buffer, 3=Modem, 4=ModemPPP.
- `parity` (Number) This is the parity to assign to the port, 0=7-None, 1=7-Even, 2=7-Odd, 3=8-None, 4=8-Even, 5=8-Odd.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `portname` (String) This is the Name of the Port that is displayed.
- `seq` (Number) This is the type of Sequence Disconnect to assign to the port, 1=Three Characters (before and after), 2=One Character Only, 3=Off
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `stopbits` (Number) This is the stop bits to assign to the port, 1=1 Stop Bit, 2=2 Stop Bit.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `tout` (Number) This is the Port Activity Timeout to assign to the port, 0=Off, 1=5 Min, 2=15 Min, 3=30 Min, 4=90 Min, 5=1 Min.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `use_https` (Boolean) Designates to use an https connection or http connection.
- `use_proxy` (Boolean) Flag to control if the lookup will observe HTTP proxy environment variables when present.
- `validate_certs` (Boolean) If false, SSL certificates will not be validated. This should only be used on personally controlled sites using self-signed certificates.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `data` (String) The output JSON returned from the commands sent
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
